Then, proceed to Document 2 for the discussion on the role of the Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI). Document 4 discusses the constitutional validity and competency of the Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) in India. Document 5 explains the recommendations of the 14th Finance Commission of India enabling the states to improve their fiscal position. Document 6 examines the jurisdiction of the Comptroller and Auditor General (CAG) in India. Document 7 analyzes the recommendations of the Attorney-General in India. title: Judiciary
text:

### Judicial Reforms and Constitutional Changes

#### Establishment and Constitution of Supreme Court

**Article 22:**
The Supreme Court was established by Article 22 of the Constitution of India. This article states that the Supreme Court shall consist of a Chief Justice and up to seven other Judges.

**Article 23:**
The Chief Justice of India shall be appointed by the President by a warrant under his hand and seal after consultation with the Chief Justice of the High Courts in the States. The President may prescribe a larger number of Judges.

**Article 24:**
Every Judge of the Supreme Court shall be appointed by the President by a warrant under his hand and seal after consultation with the Chief Justice of the High Courts in the States. The President may prescribe a larger number of Judges.

**Article 25:**
The age of a Judge of the Supreme Court shall be determined by the President by such authority and in such manner as Parliament may by law provide.

**Article 26:**
A person shall not be qualified for appointment as a Judge of the Supreme Court unless he is a citizen of India and has been for at least five years a Judge of a High Court or of two or more such Courts in succession. He must also have been for at least ten years an Advocate of a High Court or of two or more such Courts in succession.

**Article 27:**
A Judge of the Supreme Court shall not be removed from his office except by an order of the President passed after an address by each House of Parliament supported by a majority of the total membership of that House and by a majority of not less than two-thirds of the members of that House present and voting has been presented to the President in the same session for such removal on the ground of proved misbehaviour or incapacity.

**Article 28:**
Every person appointed to be a Judge of the Supreme Court shall, before he enters upon his office, make and subscribe before the President, or some person appointed in that behalf by him, an oath or affirmation according to the form set out for the purpose in the Third Schedule.

**Article 29:**
No person who has held office as a Judge of the Supreme Court shall plead or act in any court or before any authority within the territory of India.

**Article 30:**
The Chief Justice of India may at any time, with the previous consent of the President, request in writing the attendance at the sittings of the Court, as an ad hoc Judge, for such period as may be necessary, of a Judge of a High Court duly qualified for appointment as a Judge of the Supreme Court to be designated by the Chief Justice of India. Every such person so requested shall, while so sitting and acting, be entitled to such allowances as the President may by order determine and have all the jurisdiction, powers and privileges of, but shall not otherwise be deemed to be, a Judge of that Court.

**Article 31:**
The Supreme Court shall be a court of record and shall have all the powers of such a court including the power to punish for contempt of itself.

**Article 32:**
The Supreme Court shall, to the exclusion of any other court, have original jurisdiction in any dispute between the Government of India and one or more States; between the Government of India and any State or States on one side and one or more other States on the other; and between two or more States, if and in so far as the dispute involves any question (whether of law or fact) on which the existence or extent of a legal right depends.

**Article 33:**
The Supreme Court may, in its discretion, grant special leave to appeal from any judgment, decree, determination, sentence or order in any cause or matter passed or made by any court or tribunal in the territory of India.

**Article 34:**
The Supreme Court may, in its discretion, grant special leave to appeal from any judgment, decree, determination, sentence or order passed or made by any court or tribunal constituted by or under any law relating to the Armed Forces.

**Article 35:**
The Supreme Court may, in its discretion, grant special leave to appeal from any judgment, decree, determination, sentence or order passed or made by any court or tribunal constituted by or under any law relating to the Armed Forces.

title: Prime Minister
text:
Please refer to the provided documents for the above questions. Please provide your answers in bullet point format. Include references wherever necessary.

---

### Notes:

#### **Council of Ministers to Aid and Advice the President**

**Article:** (1)
- **Purpose**: To assist and advise the President in exercising his functions.
- **Limitations**: The President may require the Council of Ministers to reconsider their advice, either generally or otherwise, and the President shall act in accordance with the advice tendered after such reconsideration.
- **Disqualifications**: Members of either House of Parliament belonging to any political party who are disqualified for being a member of that House under Article 109 shall also be disqualified to be appointed as a Minister under this Article for duration of the period commencing from the date of his disqualification till the date on which the term of his office as such member would expire or where he contests any election to either House of Parliament before the expiry of such period, till the date on which he is declared elected, whichever is earlier.

**Article:** (2)
- **Total Number of Ministers**: The Prime Minister shall be appointed by the President and the other Ministers shall be appointed by the President on the advice of the Prime Minister.
- **Reconsideration Period**: The question whether any, and if so what, advice was tendered by Ministers to the President shall not be inquired into in any court.

**Article:** (3)
- **Constitutional Limitations**: The Council of Ministers shall be collectively responsible to the House of the People.
- **Appointment**: The Prime Minister shall be appointed by the President and the other Ministers shall be appointed by the President on the advice of the Prime Minister.
- **Term of Office**: The Ministers shall hold office during the pleasure of the President.

**Article:** (4)
- **Salary and Allowances**: The salaries and allowances of Ministers shall be such as Parliament may from time to time by law determine and, until Parliament so determines, shall be as specified in the Second Schedule.

**Article:** (5)
- **Orders and Instruments**: The President shall make rules for the more convenient transaction of the business of the Government of India, and for the allocation among Ministers of the said business.
- **Validity**: The validity of an order or instrument which is so authenticated shall not be called in question on the ground that it is not an order or instrument made or executed by the President.

**Article:** (6)
- **Resignation**: The salaries and allowances of Ministers shall be such as Parliament may from time to time by law determine and, until Parliament so determines, shall be as specified in the Second Schedule.

---

### Notes:

#### **Conduct of Business of the Government of India**

**Article:** 
- **All Executive Action**: All executive action of the Government of India shall be expressed to be taken in the name of the President.
- **Order and Instrument**: Orders and other instruments made and executed in the name of the President shall be authenticated in such manner as may be specified in rules to be made by the President, and the validity of an order or instrument which is so authenticated shall not be called in question on the ground that it is not an order or instrument made or executed by the President.
- **Vice-Presidents**: The Vice-President of India shall be the Chairman of the Rajya Sabha.

---

### Notes:

#### **Duties of Prime Minister as Respectively Concerning Filing of Information to the President, etc.**

**Article:** 
- **Filing of Information**: It shall be the duty of the Prime Minister to communicate to the President all decisions of the Council of Ministers relating to the administration of the affairs of the Union and proposals for legislation;
- **Filing of Information**: The Prime Minister shall furnish such information relating to the administration of the affairs of the Union and proposals for legislation as the President may call for; and
- **Filing of Information**: If the President so requires, the Prime Minister shall submit for the consideration of the Council of Ministers any matter on which a decision has been taken by a Minister but which has not been considered by the Council.

---

### Notes:

#### **Role of the Vice-Presidents of India as the Chairman of the Rajya Sabha**

**Article:** 
- **Chairman of the Rajya Sabha**: The Vice-President of India shall be the Chairman of the Rajya Sabha.

---
